name: base
description: Database management, forms, reports, and data operations with LibreOffice Base.
capabilities: Create new ODB databases with embedded HSQLDB or Firebird engines
Connect to external databases including MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQLite
Design and automate data entry forms and custom reports
Execute SQL queries and manage result sets programmatically
Import and export data from CSV files and spreadsheets
Automate LibreOffice Base via command-line and Python UNO scripts
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 25 patterns but all are false positives. External command detections are legitimate LibreOffice CLI examples. URL references point to official documentation. Cryptographic warnings are incorrect - no crypto code exists. The skill is documentation for database automation using LibreOffice Base UNO API with no security concerns.
valueStatement: Working with databases often requires complex tools and manual workflows. This skill automates database creation, form design, and report generation using LibreOffice Base and UNO scripting.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installation on the system
UNO automation needs LibreOffice running in socket mode
External database connections require appropriate JDBC or ODBC drivers
Cannot modify existing database schema without explicit user direction

useCases.targetUser: Small business owners managing inventory without dedicated IT staff
useCases.title: Small Business Inventory System
useCases.description: Create a database application to track products, suppliers, and stock levels with custom forms for data entry and automated inventory reports.

useCases.targetUser: Researchers and academics collecting and organizing study data
useCases.title: Research Data Collection
useCases.description: Build a database with structured tables for research data, design forms for consistent data entry, and generate statistical summary reports.

useCases.targetUser: Database administrators migrating systems or creating backups
useCases.title: Legacy Database Migration
useCases.description: Connect to existing MySQL or PostgreSQL databases, extract data to ODB format, and create migration documentation with schema reports.

promptTemplates.title: Create a new database
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new LibreOffice Base database for tracking customer contacts with fields for name, email, phone, company, and notes. Save it as contacts.odb.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new database project from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Connect to external database
promptTemplates.prompt: Connect LibreOffice Base to my MySQL database at localhost:3306 named 'sales' using username 'admin'. Create linked tables for the customers and orders tables.
promptTemplates.scenario: Working with existing external database systems

promptTemplates.title: Design a data entry form
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a form for the products table with labeled input fields, a navigation bar for browsing records, and buttons for adding, editing, and deleting entries.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building user interfaces for database interaction

promptTemplates.title: Generate automated report
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a report that shows monthly sales summary grouped by product category, including total revenue, units sold, and top performing products with charts.

bestPractices: Always use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection when executing dynamic SQL
Create indexes on frequently queried columns to improve database performance
Store database source files in version control and maintain regular backup copies
antiPatterns: Avoid storing plain text passwords in database connection strings - use credential managers
Do not embed database files directly in shared folders without access controls
Never skip transaction handling when performing multiple related data operations

faq.question: What database engines does LibreOffice Base support?
faq.answer: Base supports embedded HSQLDB and Firebird engines, plus connections to MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and any ODBC or JDBC compatible database system.

faq.question: Do I need to install LibreOffice to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, LibreOffice must be installed on your system. For UNO automation, LibreOffice needs to run in socket mode using the --headless --accept socket command.

faq.question: Can this skill convert Access databases to Base format?
faq.answer: Direct conversion is not supported. You can export Access data to CSV or MySQL, then import into Base, or use ODBC to connect Base to Access files.

faq.question: How do I share a Base database with multiple users?
faq.answer: For multi-user access, connect Base to a server database like MySQL or PostgreSQL rather than using embedded HSQLDB. The embedded engine is single-user only.

faq.question: What is the difference between HSQLDB and Firebird in Base?
faq.answer: Firebird is the newer embedded engine with better performance and modern SQL features. HSQLDB is legacy but still supported. New databases default to Firebird.

faq.question: Can I run Base databases headless on a server?
faq.answer: Yes, using LibreOffice in headless mode with UNO scripting. The database file remains accessible via the socket connection for automated operations.
